name: Password in Configuration File
description: The product stores a password in a configuration file that might be accessible to unauthorized actors. An attacker gaining access to this file can learn the stored password or modify it, compromising the system the password protects.
detection_pattern: Look for STORAGE nodes (configuration files, properties files, XML configs, .env files) containing AUTH data (plaintext passwords, credentials) that are accessible without a CONTROL mediator (file permission enforcement, encryption at rest, secrets manager) that restricts access and protects the stored credential from unauthorized reading.
